q: I don't know. + Will my friend call me or not?
o: 1. I don't know whether my friend will call me or not.
2. I don't know whether will my friend call me or not.

q: Do you know? + Is Tom married?
o: 1. Do you know if Tom married?
2. Do you know if Tom is married?

q: I wonder. + Can I go there?
o: 1. I wonder can I go there.
2. I wonder whether I can go there.

q: Do you know? + What happened last night?
o: 1. Do you know what happened last night?
2. Do you know what did happen last night?

q: I'm not sure. + Is the rumor true or not?
o: 1. I'm not sure whether the rumor is true or not.
2. I'm not sure if the rumor is true or not.

q: It doesn't matter. + Will she come or not?
o: 1. Whether she will come or not doesn’t matter to me.
2. If she will come or not doesn’t matter to me.

q: Can you tell me? + When will Jane come?
o: 1. Can you tell me when Jane will come.
2. When can you tell me Jane will come.

q: I doubt. + Does she love me?
o: 1. I doubt if she love me.
2. I doubt if she loves me.

q: Do you think? + When does the movie start?
o: 1. Do you think when the movie starts?
2. When do you think the movie starts?

q: I wonder. + Will he win the race?
o: 1. I wonder if will he win the race.
2. I wonder if he will win the race.

q: I don't know. + Did he steal my pen?
o: 1. I don't know if he steal my pen.
2. I don't know if he stole my pen.

q: Do you know? + What did Jane do?
o: 1. Do you know what did Jane do?
2. Do you know what Jane did?

q: I doubt. + Did he say such a rude word?
o: 1. I doubt whether he says such a rude word.
2. I doubt whether he said such a rude word.
